source.title: Compact deep neural network models of visual cortex

confidence_rationale: Strong empirical support for shared-basis + consolidation motif in macaque V4. Transfer risk to general cognition is real but structurally coherent. Confidence capped at 0.72 to reflect species/modality gap and the inferential step from sensory cortex to full cognitive architecture.

mapping.source_claim_statement: V4 neurons share an early filter bank; stimulus preference is determined by a neuron-specific consolidation step (selective pooling + competition + normalization). Accurate models can be compressed ~5000x without accuracy loss.
mapping.ree_translation: E1 provides a shared representational basis; behavioural selectivity emerges at the consolidation/gating layer (E3 + control plane), not at the feature basis. Viable REE architectures must admit large compression without functional collapse.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Paper studies macaque V4 (mid-level visual cortex). Extension to full cognitive architecture — E3 commitment gating, ethical residue, trajectory selection — is inferential. Canonical operations (WTA, divisive normalization, surround suppression) are well-established cortical motifs and transfer risk is lower for those sub-claims.
mapping.source_context: Macaque electrophysiology, V4 responses to natural and synthetic images, ~78k unique stimuli, 3 animals.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality high: large-scale empirical study with causal validation via adversarial stimulus synthesis. Mapping fidelity good for E1-shared-basis and canonical-ops claims; lower for E3 commitment specifics. Transfer risk non-trivial: sensory cortex to full cognitive architecture is a substantive inferential step.
